title: Number Systems: Binary and Hexadecimal
summary: All computer data is stored in binary. This lesson covers binary number conversion, hexadecimal as a shorthand, and bitwise operations on multi-bit values.
content_html:
Why Different Number Systems?

Different contexts call for different representations:

SystemBaseDigitsUsed for
Decimal100-9Human-readable values
Binary20-1Hardware, machine code
Hexadecimal160-9, A-FMemory addresses, colour codes
Octal80-7Unix file permissions

Binary to Decimal Conversion

Multiply each bit by its positional weight (power of 2) and sum:

01001110₂ = 0+64+0+0+8+4+2+0 = 78₁₀

Worked Examples:

BinaryCalculationDecimal
01110₂8+4+2 = 14
10011216+2+1 = 19
10000000₂128 = 128

Decimal to Binary Conversion

Repeatedly divide by 2 and record remainders (read bottom to top):

78 ÷ 2 = 39 r0 | 39 ÷ 2 = 19 r1 | 19 ÷ 2 = 9 r1 | 9 ÷ 2 = 4 r1 | 4 ÷ 2 = 2 r0 | 2 ÷ 2 = 1 r0 | 1 ÷ 2 = 0 r1 → 01001110₂

Hexadecimal Conversion

Group binary into 4-bit nibbles and convert each group:

01001110₂ → 0100|1110 → 4|E → 0x4E

Analogue vs Digital

AnalogueDigital
Continuous values (real numbers)Discrete values (whole numbers)
Voltage, height, temperatureClass size, CD samples, pixels
Sensitive to noiseRobust to noise
Accurate for real-world measurementsEasy to store, transmit, and compute
ADC/DAC: Analogue-to-Digital Converters (ADC) sample the real world into binary. More bits = more precision. A 16-bit audio sample has 65,536 possible levels.

exercises.title: Hex representation
exercises.options_json: ["It's closer to decimal", "Each hex digit maps to exactly 4 bits", "It uses fewer digits than octal", "It's compatible with all CPUs"]
exercises.answer: Each hex digit maps to exactly 4 bits
